summary: Regenerated two large gm assembly files, gm_1A36.s and gmmain_lib.s, so their assembly listings use real linked addresses instead of zero-based offsets and cleaner regenerated data directives. The companion symbols.txt changes adjusted several inferred data kinds, notably gmMainLib_803D4A60 from double to 8byte and several gm sdata2 8-byte entries from double to 4byte. There were no PR body notes or review comments, so intent beyond regeneration is inferred from the title and diff.
what_changed: Regenerated asm/melee/gm/gm_1A36.s, a very large assembly file, with comments and labels based on the linked text address range 0x801A3680-0x801BFFA8 instead of a zero-based 0x00000000-0x0001C928 range.
Regenerated asm/melee/gm/gmmain_lib.s with linked text addresses 0x8015CC34-0x8015FD24 instead of zero-based offsets.
Updated local branch labels in regenerated assembly from zero-offset names such as .L_00000084 to address-based names such as .L_801A3704.

Updated section range comments for data-bearing sections, for example gm_1A36 .data from 0x803DA788-0x803DFEA8, .bss from 0x80479C30-0x8049E6C8, .sdata from 0x804D4268-0x804D44E0, .sbss from 0x804D6720-0x804D6950, and .sdata2 from 0x804DA9E8-0x804DAC90.

Cleaned regenerated data representation in gm_1A36.s by converting many hex .4byte sequences into explicit .string directives, including filenames such as "MvEndDonkey.mth", "MvEndMewtwo.mth", and many "GmRegendSimple*.thp" strings.

Cleaned regenerated data representation in gmmain_lib.s by converting gmMainLib_803D4A60 from eight .4byte lines into four .8byte lines and representing gmMainLib_804DA228 as .float 100 instead of .4byte 0x42C80000.

Updated config/GALE01/symbols.txt data-kind annotations for 11 symbol rows, including gmMainLib_803D4A60 from data:double to data:8byte and several gm_804DA9F0/gm_804DAA80/gm_804DAA90/gm_804DAAB8/gm_804DAAC0/gm_804DAB80/gm_804DAB88/gm_804DABB8/gm_804DABC0/gm_804DAC10 entries from data:double to data:4byte.
smart_moves: Kept the regeneration scoped to two gm assembly translation-unit slices plus the symbol metadata needed to explain the regenerated data directives.

Preserved existing address-based function names such as gm_801A3680, gm_801A36A0, gm_801A36C0, gm_801A36E0, gm_801A3714, gm_801A3820, and gmMainLib_8015FCC0 while improving generated labels and comments.
Used linked addresses in instruction comments, which makes branches, relocations, and symbol references easier to compare against maps and disassembly tools than zero-based assembly offsets.
Allowed regeneration to expose semantically clearer data directives: strings are emitted as .string, the 100.0 constant as .float 100, and packed 64-bit literal data as .8byte where appropriate.
decomp_lessons: When regenerating asm dumps, address bases matter: replacing zero-based comments and labels with real linked addresses makes the output more searchable and easier to align with symbols.txt and map data.

Do not assume every 8-byte object is a double. This PR corrected several size-0x8 sdata2 symbols away from data:double, showing that symbol data kind must match actual usage or generator output rather than size alone.
String recognition in generated assembly is useful decomp groundwork: .string directives make rodata/data tables readable and expose asset names that can guide later C reconstruction.

Regenerated assembly may be mostly mechanical but can still carry important metadata corrections in symbols.txt; future reviewers should inspect symbol kind changes separately from huge address-comment churn.
Large asm refreshes are easier to reason about when limited to generated files and the exact config metadata that changed generation behavior.
naming_conventions: Unidentified global functions and objects retain address-derived names such as gm_801A3680, gm_80479C30, gmMainLib_803D4A60, and gmMainLib_804DA228.
Local assembly labels in regenerated output use linked-address suffixes, e.g. .L_801A3704, instead of zero-offset suffixes such as .L_00000084.
Generated gap labels continue to include section/address context, e.g. gap_09_804D3EE5_sdata and gap_11_804DA22C_sdata2.

Asset string contents preserve original game naming and spelling, including examples like GmRegendSimpleGamewatch.thp and GmRegendSimpleZeldaseak.thp; no evidence in this PR suggests renaming those strings.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Regenerate assembly with linked virtual addresses in both section headers and per-instruction comments to improve diffability against the canonical DOL address space.
Use address-based local labels after regen so branch targets remain directly searchable by runtime address.
Prefer semantic data directives produced by the generator, such as .string and .float, when they reflect the underlying bytes and symbol metadata.
Keep symbols.txt data-kind annotations synchronized with generated assembly output so future C extraction and asm regeneration do not fight stale type guesses.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that decompile gm_1A36 functions after this regeneration.
Search for uses or struct interpretations of gm_80479C30 around gm_801A3680 and related accessors.
Check whether the corrected sdata2 data:4byte symbols were later renamed or typed more precisely.
Look for subsequent PRs using the GmRegendSimple*.thp strings to identify ending/movie table structures.
Compare generator changes around February 2024 that caused linked-address asm output and semantic data directives.
